Overview

Address
466 FLAGSHIP ROAD, NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92663

9496428044

143 (86% of beds filled)

For-profit LLC
Provider type

1978-05-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.90 4.60 3.28 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.38 0.80 0.32 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.30 1.02 1.09 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.23 2.77 1.87 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.67 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.69
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.32
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.10
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
42.5%
annual
RN turnover
50.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.63
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.18
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-08-19 39 19 20 116 1 116
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-07-19 37 24 17 136 1 136
Total weighted health score
121.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (89)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

17 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
72 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-10-06 2025-11-05
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-10-06 2025-11-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. B 2025-10-06 2025-11-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-10-06 2025-11-05
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. B 2025-10-06 2025-11-05
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. B 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. B 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. B 2025-09-25 2025-10-25
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. B 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. B 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. B 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-08-19 2025-09-18
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. B 2025-07-09 2025-08-08
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. B 2025-07-09 2025-08-08
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-04-18 2025-05-18
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. B 2025-02-18 2025-03-20
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-01-29 2025-02-28
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-07-19 2024-08-19

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
